name: Unit
javadoc: /** * Represents all pieces that can be moved on the map-board. This includes: * colonists, ships, wagon trains e.t.c. * * Every Unit is owned by a {@link Player} and has a * {@link Location}. */

members.name: Unit
members.javadoc: /** * Constructor for ServerUnit. * * @param game The enclosing Game. */

tags.name: game
tags.content: The enclosing Game.

members.name: Unit
members.javadoc: /** * Initialize this object from an XML-representation of this object. * * @param game The enclosing Game. * @param e An XML-element that will be used to initialize this object. */

tags.name: game
tags.content: The enclosing Game.

tags.name: e
tags.content: An XML-element that will be used to initialize this object.

members.name: Unit
members.javadoc: /** * Creates a new Unit with the given * identifier. The object should later be initialized by calling * {@link #readFromXML(FreeColXMLReader)}. * * @param game The enclosing Game. * @param id The object identifier. */

tags.name: game
tags.content: The enclosing Game.

tags.name: id
tags.content: The object identifier.

members.name: getLabel
members.javadoc: /** * Get a plain string template for a unit. * * @return The StringTemplate to describe the given unit. */

tags.content: The StringTemplate to describe the given unit.

members.name: getLabel
members.javadoc: /** * Get a string template for a unit. * * The PLAIN template contains: * - The type of the unit * - A role if not the default * - The specific name of the unit if it has one * The NATIONAL template adds the nation * The FULL template adds equipment annotations * * @param ult The type of label to get. * @return The StringTemplate to describe the given unit. */

tags.name: ult
tags.content: The type of label to get.
The StringTemplate to describe the given unit.

members.name: getDescription
members.javadoc: /** * Get the basic i18n description for this unit. * * @return A String describing this unit. */

tags.content: A String describing this unit.

members.name: getDescription
members.javadoc: /** * Get the basic i18n description for this unit. * * @param ult The label type required. * @return A String describing this unit. */

tags.name: ult
tags.content: The label type required.
A String describing this unit.

tags.name: tile

A suitable label.

members.name: isPerson
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Checks if this unit is a person, that is not a ship or wagon.
 * Surprisingly difficult without explicit enumeration because
 * model.ability.person only arrived in 0.10.1.
 *
 * @return True if this unit is a person.
 */

tags.content: True if this unit is a person.

members.name: getState
members.javadoc: /** * Gets the state of this Unit. * * @return The state of this Unit. */

tags.content: The state of this Unit.

members.name: checkSetState
members.javadoc: /** * Checks if a Unit can get the given state set. * * @param s The new state for this Unit. Should be one of * {UnitState.ACTIVE, FORTIFIED, ...}. * @return True if the Unit state can be changed to * the new value. */

tags.name: s
tags.content:
The new state for this Unit.  Should be one of
    {UnitState.ACTIVE, FORTIFIED, ...}.
True if the Unit state can be changed to the new value.

members.name: setState
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Sets a new state for this unit and initializes the amount of
 * work the unit has left.
 *
 * If the work needs turns to be completed (for instance when
 * plowing), then the moves the unit has still left will be used
 * up. Some work (basically building a road with a hardy pioneer)
 * might actually be finished already in this method-call, in
 * which case the state is set back to UnitState.ACTIVE.
 *
 * @param s The new state for this Unit.  Should be one of
 *     {UnitState.ACTIVE, UnitState.FORTIFIED, ...}.
 */

tags.name: s
tags.content:
The new state for this Unit.  Should be one of
    {UnitState.ACTIVE, UnitState.FORTIFIED, ...}.

members.name: setStateUnchecked
members.javadoc: /** * Actually set the unit state. * * @param s The new UnitState. */

tags.name: s
tags.content: The new UnitState.

members.name: setStateToAllChildren
members.javadoc: /** * Sets the given state to all the units that are carried. * * @param state The UnitState to set.. */

tags.name: state
tags.content: The UnitState to set..

members.name: changeOwner
members.javadoc: /** * Change the owner of this unit. * * -vis: This routine calls setOwner() and thus has visibility * implications. Ideally it should be in ServerUnit but we keep * it here for the benefit of the test suite. * * @param owner The new owner Player. */

tags.name: owner
tags.content: The new owner Player.

members.name: getNeededTurnsOfTraining
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets the number of turns this unit has to train to educate a student.
 * This value is only meaningful for units that can be put in a school.
 *
 * @return The turns of training needed to teach its current type
 *     to a free colonist or to promote an indentured servant or a
 *     petty criminal.
 * @see #getTurnsOfTraining
 */

tags.content:
The turns of training needed to teach its current type
    to a free colonist or to promote an indentured servant or a
    petty criminal.
#getTurnsOfTraining

members.name: getNationality
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets the nationality of this Unit.
 *
 * Nationality represents a Unit's personal allegiance to a
 * nation.  This may conflict with who currently issues orders to
 * the Unit (the owner).
 *
 * @return The nationality of this Unit.
 */

tags.content: The nationality of this Unit.

members.name: setNationality
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Sets the nationality of this Unit.  A unit will change
 * nationality when it switches owners willingly.  Currently only
 * Converts do this, but it opens the possibility of
 * naturalisation.
 *
 * @param newNationality The new nationality of this Unit.
 */

tags.name: newNationality
tags.content: The new nationality of this Unit.

members.name: getEthnicity
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets the ethnicity of this Unit.
 *
 * Ethnicity is inherited from the inhabitants of the place where
 * the Unit was born.  Allows former converts to become
 * native-looking colonists.
 *
 * @return The ethnicity of this Unit.
 */

tags.content: The ethnicity of this Unit.

members.name: setEthnicity
members.javadoc:
/**

tags.name: newEthnicity

members.name: isDefensiveUnit
members.javadoc: /** * Checks if this is an defensive unit. That is: a unit which can * be used to defend a Settlement. * * Note! As this method is used by the AI it really means that the * unit can defend as is. To be specific an unarmed colonist is * not defensive yet, even if Paul Revere and stockpiled muskets * are available. That check is only performed on an actual * attack. * * A settlement is lost when there are no more defensive units. * * @return True if this is a defensive unit meaning it can be used * to defend a Colony. This would normally mean * that a defensive unit also will be offensive. */

tags.content: True if this is a defensive unit meaning it can be used to defend a Colony. This would normally mean that a defensive unit also will be offensive.

members.code: /** * Checks if this is an defensive unit. That is: a unit which can * be used to defend a Settlement. * * Note! As this method is used by the AI it really means that the * unit can defend as is. To be specific an unarmed colonist is * not defensive yet, even if Paul Revere and stockpiled muskets * are available. That check is only performed on an actual * attack. * * A settlement is lost when there are no more defensive units. * * @return True if this is a defensive unit meaning it can be used * to defend a Colony. This would normally mean * that a defensive unit also will be offensive. */ public boolean isDefensiveUnit() { return (unitType.isDefensive() || getRole().isDefensive()) && // Not wagons or ships !isCarrier(); }

members.name: shouldTakeTransportTo
members.javadoc: /** * Should the unit use transport to get to a specified tile? * * True if: * - The location is not null * - The unit is not naval * - The unit is not there already * AND * - there is no path OR the path uses an existing carrier * * @param loc The Location to go to. * @return True if the unit should use transport. */

tags.content: The Location to go to.
True if the unit should use transport.

members.code: /** * Should the unit use transport to get to a specified tile? * * True if: * - The location is not null * - The unit is not naval * - The unit is not there already * AND * - there is no path OR the path uses an existing carrier * * @param loc The Location to go to. * @return True if the unit should use transport. */ public boolean shouldTakeTransportTo(Location loc) { PathNode path; return loc != null && !isNaval() && !isAtLocation(loc) && ((path = findPath(getLocation(), loc, getCarrier(), null)) == null || path.usesCarrier()); }

members.name: getTrivialPath

members.name: findPath
members.javadoc: /** * Finds the fastest path from the current location to the * specified one. No carrier is provided, and the default cost * decider for this unit is used. * * @param end The Location in which the path ends. * @return A PathNode from the current location to the * end location, or null if none found. */

A PathNode from the current location to the end location, or null if none found.

members.name: findPath
members.javadoc: /** * Finds a quickest path between specified locations, optionally * using a carrier and special purpose cost decider. * * @param start The Location to start at. * @param end The Location to end at. * @param carrier An optional carrier Unit to carry the unit. * @param costDecider An optional CostDecider for * determining the movement costs (uses default cost deciders * for the unit/s if not provided). * @return A PathNode, or null if no path is found. */

tags.name: start
tags.content: The Location to start at.

tags.name: end
tags.content: The Location to end at.

tags.name: carrier
tags.content: An optional carrier Unit to carry the unit.

tags.name: costDecider
tags.content: An optional CostDecider for determining the movement costs (uses default cost deciders for the unit/s if not provided).
A PathNode, or null if no path is found.

members.code: /** * Finds a quickest path between specified locations, optionally * using a carrier and special purpose cost decider. * * @param start The Location to start at. * @param end The Location to end at. * @param carrier An optional carrier Unit to carry the unit. * @param costDecider An optional CostDecider for * determining the movement costs (uses default cost deciders * for the unit/s if not provided). * @return A PathNode, or null if no path is found. */ public PathNode findPath(Location start, Location end, Unit carrier, CostDecider costDecider) { return getGame().getMap().findPath(this, start, end, carrier, costDecider, null); }

members.name: findPathToNeighbour
members.javadoc: /** * Finds a quickest path to a neighbouring tile to a specified target * tile, optionally using a carrier and special purpose cost decider. * * @param start The Location to start at. * @param end The Tile to end at a neighbour of. * @param carrier An optional carrier Unit to carry the unit. * @param costDecider An optional CostDecider for * determining the movement costs (uses default cost deciders * for the unit/s if not provided). * @return A PathNode, or null if no path is found. */

tags.name: costDecider
tags.content: An optional CostDecider for determining the movement costs (uses default cost deciders for the unit/s if not provided).
A PathNode, or null if no path is found.

The number of turns it will take to reach the destination, or MANY_TURNS if no path can be found.

members.name: getTurnsToReach
members.javadoc: /** * Gets the number of turns required for this unit to reach a * destination location from a starting location, using an optional * carrier and cost decider. * * @param start The Location to start the search from. * @param end The destination Location. * @param carrier An optional carrier Unit to use. * @param costDecider An optional CostDecider to * score the path with. * @return The number of turns it will take to reach the end, * or MANY_TURNS if no path can be found. */

members.code: /** * Gets the number of turns required for this unit to reach a * destination location from a starting location, using an optional * carrier and cost decider. * * @param start The Location to start the search from. * @param end The destination Location. * @param carrier An optional carrier Unit to use. * @param costDecider An optional CostDecider to * score the path with. * @return The number of turns it will take to reach the end, * or MANY_TURNS if no path can be found. */ public int getTurnsToReach(Location start, Location end, Unit carrier, CostDecider costDecider) { PathNode path = findPath(start, end, carrier, costDecider); return (path == null) ? MANY_TURNS : path.getTotalTurns(); }

members.code: /** * Convenience wrapper for the * {@link net.sf.freecol.common.model.Map#search} function. * * @param start The Location to start the search from. * @param gd The object responsible for determining whether a * given PathNode is a goal or not. * @param cd An optional CostDecider * responsible for determining the path cost. * @param maxTurns The maximum number of turns the given * Unit is allowed to move. This is the * maximum search range for a goal. * @param carrier An optional naval carrier Unit to use. * @return The path to a goal, or null if none can be found. */ public PathNode search(Location start, GoalDecider gd, CostDecider cd, int maxTurns, Unit carrier) { return (start == null) ? null : getGame().getMap().search(this, start, gd, cd, maxTurns, carrier, null); }

members.name: getPioneerScore
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Score this unit with its suitability for pioneering.
 *
 * A pioneer must be a colonst.  Favour:
 * - existing pioneers especially if on the map
 * - expert pioneer units
 * - then by skill but not other experts
 *
 * @return A pioneering score.
 */

tags.content: A pioneering score.

members.name: getScoutScore
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Score this unit with its suitability for scouting.
 *
 * A scout must be a colonist.  Favour:
 * - existing scouts especially if on the map
 * - expert scouts
 * - lower skill level as scouting is a good career for crims and servants
 *   which might become seasoned scouts
 *
 * @return A scouting score.
 */

tags.content: A scouting score.
